message:
feat(db,storage): fix conditional compilation for tantivy benches and harden storage path security

Fix search_v3_bench.rs compilation when tantivy-engine feature is disabled:
- Replace crate-level #![cfg(feature = "tantivy-engine")] with per-item
  #[cfg(feature = "tantivy-engine")] attributes on every function, struct,
  const, and criterion macro invocation
- Add #[cfg(not(feature = "tantivy-engine"))] fn main() {} fallback so the
  benchmark binary always compiles

Harden mcp-agent-mail-storage path handling and security:
- Decouple archive process lock from archive reference with new
  archive_process_lock_for_root(root: PathBuf)
- archive_process_lock() now returns Result<Arc<Mutex<()>>> using
  archive_project_root_canonical_checked()
- with_project_lock() uses archive_project_root_checked() for lock path
- All file operations (write_project_metadata, write_agent_profile,
  write_file_reservation_records, message_paths, update_thread_digest,
  store_attachment, store_raw_attachment, list_agent_inbox) now use
  validated/canonical project root paths
- All git operations (get_recent_commits, find_commit_for_path,
  get_commits_by_author, get_recent_commits_extended, get_commit_detail,
  get_archive_tree, get_archive_file_content, get_historical_inbox_snapshot,
  get_communication_graph, get_timeline_commits) now use
  open_archive_repo_checked(), validate_repo_relative_path(),
  validate_archive_component(), and/or reject_symlinked_archive_root()

Update Cargo.lock with libc dependency from ftui-core workspace member.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
